sql: SELECT Nationality, AVG(Age) AS Average_Age FROM pilot GROUP BY Nationality;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique names of pilots who have flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT T1.Model, T1.Manufacturer, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS NumberOfFlights FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Model, T1.Manufacturer;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each aircraft model and its manufacturer, along with the total count of flights recorded for that model.

sql: SELECT T1.Manufacturer, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Pilot_ID) AS NumberOfPilots FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID JOIN pilot AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Manufacturer HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Pilot_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing aircraft manufacturers whose planes have been flown by more than one distinct pilot, along with the total count of those pilots.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID WHERE T2.Aircraft_ID IN (SELECT Aircraft_ID FROM aircraft WHERE Order_Year = 1992);

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Rank FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pilot_record AS T2 WHERE T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID);

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Age, T2.FlightCount FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Pilot_ID, COUNT(*) AS FlightCount FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID WHERE T1.Age > 35;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows for pilots older than 35, listing their name, age, and the total number of flights they have recorded.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Rank FROM pilot WHERE Rank > (SELECT AVG(Rank) FROM pilot);

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' AND T3.Fuel_Propulsion = 'Diesel';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names of pilots from the United States who have a record of flying a diesel-powered aircraft.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' UNION SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Model = 'Phantom (High Floor)';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of pilots who are either from the United States or have flown the 'Phantom (High Floor)' aircraft model.

sql: SELECT T1.Team, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS Flight_Count FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Team ORDER BY Flight_Count DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the top 3 pilot teams with the highest total flight counts, ordered from most flights to least.

sql: SELECT T1.Manufacturer, T1.Model FROM aircraft AS T1 WHERE T1.Aircraft_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Aircraft_ID FROM pilot AS T3 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T3.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Team = 'Bradley');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the manufacturer and model of aircraft that have not been flown by any pilot from the 'Bradley' team.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pilot_record AS T2 WHERE T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any pilot who does not have an associated flight record.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Team, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS RecordCount FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_ID, T1.Pilot_name, T1.Team HAVING COUNT(T2.Record_ID) = (SELECT COUNT(Record_ID) FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID ORDER BY COUNT(Record_ID) DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the pilot with the most records, showing their name, team, and the total count of those records.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig' INTERSECT SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Rank > 10;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of high-ranking pilots (rank greater than 10) who have also flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_ID, T1.Pilot_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Model) >= 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a pilot who has flown at least two distinct aircraft models.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.name = 'University of Oxford';

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T2.authID) FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID, T1.name;

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 WHERE T1.authID IN (SELECT T2.authID FROM Authorship AS T2 JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'UK');

sql: SELECT T3.fname, T3.lname, T4.fname, T4.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID AND T1.authID < T2.authID JOIN Authors AS T3 ON T1.authID = T3.authID JOIN Authors AS T4 ON T2.authID = T4.authID;

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID HAVING COUNT(paperID) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the unique name of an institution affiliated with authors who have published more than one paper.

sql: SELECT T1.title, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.country) FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID GROUP BY T1.paperID, T1.title HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.country) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each paper's title along with a count of the number of distinct countries associated with its authors' institutions.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Authorship AS T2 WHERE T1.authID = T2.authID) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Authorship AS T3 WHERE T1.authID = T3.authID AND T3.authOrder > 1);

sql: SELECT name FROM Inst WHERE country = 'USA' UNION SELECT name FROM Inst WHERE country = 'UK';

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'UK' EXCEPT SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'USA';

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T1.authID, T1.fname, T1.lname ORDER BY COUNT(T2.paperID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of the author who has written the most papers.

sql: SELECT T3.title FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T2.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T1.lname IN ('Gibbons', 'Hinze') GROUP BY T3.title HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.authID) = 2;

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.instID = 1000 GROUP BY T1.authID, T2.fname, T2.lname HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.paperID) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT paperID) FROM Authorship WHERE instID = 1000);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the first and last name of the author from institution 1000 who has contributed to every paper published by that institution.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname, T3.title FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T2.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T2.paperID = (SELECT MAX(T4.paperID) FROM Authorship AS T4 WHERE T4.authID = T1.authID);
result_summary: Returns 13 rows, listing each author's full name alongside the title of their most recent paper (determined by the highest paper ID for that author).

sql: SELECT AVG(T.num_authors) FROM (SELECT COUNT(T2.authID) AS num_authors FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID GROUP BY T1.paperID) AS T;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of authors per paper across all papers in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.instID = 1000 INTERSECT SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID;

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM Papers AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID GROUP BY T1.paperID, T1.title HAVING COUNT(T2.authID) > (SELECT AVG(T.num_authors) FROM (SELECT COUNT(authID) AS num_authors FROM Authorship GROUP BY paperID) AS T);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the title of a paper that has more authors than the average number of authors per paper in the dataset.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS number_of_flights FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS number_of_flights FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name;

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name HAVING count(T1.id) > 1;

sql: SELECT T2.name, AVG(T1.Velocity) AS avg_velocity FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name HAVING AVG(T1.Velocity) > 200;

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) AS flight_count FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id WHERE T2.Group_Equity_Shareholding > 50 GROUP BY T2.name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Region_id) AS NumberOfRegionsAffected FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id WHERE T2.Number_city_affected = (SELECT MAX(Number_city_affected) FROM affected_region);

sql: SELECT T3.Region_name FROM region AS T3 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T3.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Storm_ID = 1 EXCEPT SELECT T3.Region_name FROM region AS T3 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T3.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Storm_ID = 3;

sql: SELECT T4.Name, T4.Region_name FROM (SELECT T1.Name, T3.Region_name, T1.Number_Deaths, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY T3.Region_name ORDER BY T1.Number_Deaths DESC) as rk FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID JOIN region AS T3 ON T2.Region_id = T3.Region_id) AS T4 WHERE T4.rk = 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the deadliest storm by number of deaths for each region, showing the storm's name and the region's name.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Storm_ID IN (SELECT Storm_ID FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID HAVING AVG(Number_city_affected) > 12);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of storms that affected an average of more than 12 cities.

sql: SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths > 10 UNION SELECT Name FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD > 10.75;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of storms that either caused more than 10 deaths or resulted in over $10.75 million in damages.

sql: SELECT customer_name, customer_email FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and email address for all customers whose payment method is 'Credit Card'.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_price FROM Products WHERE product_type_code = 'Clothes';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and price for each product classified as 'Clothes'.

sql: SELECT staff_name FROM Staff;
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the name of every staff member.

sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date FROM Customer_Orders WHERE order_status_code = 'New';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the order ID, customer ID, and order date for all customer orders with a 'New' status.

sql: SELECT supplier_name, supplier_phone FROM Suppliers WHERE supplier_name = 'Lidl';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the supplier name and phone number for the specific supplier 'Lidl'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, COUNT(T2.order_id) AS order_count FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each customer's name and the total number of orders they have placed.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each supplier's name and the corresponding total value of all products purchased from them.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T3.supplier_name FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T3 ON T2.supplier_id = T3.supplier_id WHERE T1.product_type_code = 'Clothes';
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing clothing products alongside the names of their suppliers.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name, COUNT(T3.staff_id) AS number_of_staff FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id JOIN Staff AS T3 ON T2.staff_id = T3.staff_id GROUP BY T1.department_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each department name alongside a count of the total number of staff assigned to that department.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique names and email addresses of all customers who have placed at least one completed order.

sql: SELECT T1.order_id, T1.order_date, COUNT(T2.product_id) AS product_count FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id GROUP BY T1.order_id, T1.order_date;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows, providing the order ID, order date, and a count of the total number of products for each customer order.

sql: SELECT T3.department_name, T2.store_name FROM Department_Store_Chain AS T1 JOIN Department_Stores AS T2 ON T1.dept_store_chain_id = T2.dept_store_chain_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.dept_store_id = T3.dept_store_id WHERE T1.dept_store_chain_name = 'South';
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.customer_name, T1.customer_email FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'black jeans';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and email of the unique customer who has purchased the product 'black jeans'.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_price = (SELECT MAX(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of the suppliers who provide the most expensive product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed' GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_name HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 1;
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T4.product_price) AS total_spent_on_completed_orders FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed' GROUP BY T1.customer_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each customer's name and the total amount they have spent, calculated by summing the product prices from all of their completed orders.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id JOIN Departments AS T3 ON T2.department_id = T3.department_id WHERE T2.job_title_code = 'Department Manager' AND T3.department_name = 'human resource';
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the staff member who is the Department Manager for the Human Resources department.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, T4.address_details FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.supplier_id = T3.supplier_id JOIN Addresses AS T4 ON T3.address_id = T4.address_id GROUP BY T1.supplier_name, T4.address_details HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.product_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names and address details for suppliers who provide more than one distinct product.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, T4.supplier_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id JOIN Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.supplier_id = T4.supplier_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing pairs of customers and suppliers who share the same physical address.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.product_price FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id NOT IN (SELECT product_id FROM Order_Items);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing products that have never been ordered, showing each product's name and price.

sql: SELECT T1.department_name, COUNT(T2.staff_id) AS staff_count FROM Departments AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_name ORDER BY staff_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the department with the largest staff count, showing the department name and the total number of staff.

sql: SELECT customer_name AS person_name, 'Customer' AS person_type FROM Customers WHERE payment_method_code = 'Credit Card' UNION SELECT staff_name AS person_name, 'Staff' AS person_type FROM Staff WHERE staff_name = 'Tom';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows that combine two groups of people: customers who pay by 'Credit Card' and the staff member named 'Tom', showing each person's name and a type indicating if they are a 'Customer' or 'Staff'.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'red jeans' EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'yellow jeans';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a customer who has purchased 'red jeans' but has not purchased 'yellow jeans'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, AVG(T1.product_price) as avg_price FROM Products AS T1 GROUP BY T1.product_type_code HAVING AVG(T1.product_price) > (SELECT AVG(product_price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying product categories whose average price is higher than the overall average price across all products, showing the product type code and its calculated average price.

sql: SELECT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.staff_id GROUP BY T1.staff_id, T1.staff_name HAVING COUNT(T2.department_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of staff members who are assigned to more than one department.

sql: SELECT Title, Original_air_date FROM film WHERE Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the title and original air date for all films directed by Bill Schreiner.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM cinema WHERE Capacity > 500;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location for all cinemas with a capacity greater than 500.

sql: SELECT Title, Rank_in_series FROM film ORDER BY Rank_in_series DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing film titles and their rank within a series, ordered from highest to lowest rank.

sql: SELECT Cinema_ID, Film_ID, Date FROM schedule WHERE Price = 12.99;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the cinema, film, and date for all scheduled showings that are priced at 12.99.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year = 2010;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and location for all cinemas that opened in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT T2.Title FROM schedule AS T1 JOIN film AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Cinema_ID = 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the titles of all films scheduled to be shown at the cinema with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Film_ID) FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each cinema by name and the total number of films on its schedule.

sql: SELECT Date, AVG(Price) FROM schedule GROUP BY Date;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the average price of scheduled items, grouped by each unique date.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and location of each unique cinema showing a movie on '21 May'.

sql: SELECT Title FROM film WHERE Film_ID IN (SELECT Film_ID FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the titles of all films that currently have a scheduled showing.

sql: SELECT T2.Title, SUM(T1.Show_times_per_day) FROM schedule AS T1 JOIN film AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T2.Title;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each film title along with the total number of times it is shown per day across all schedules.

sql: SELECT Name FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year IN (SELECT Openning_year FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year > 2010);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all cinemas that opened after the year 2010.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Name, T1.Location, T1.Capacity FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T3.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name, location, and capacity for each unique cinema showing a film directed by 'Bill Schreiner'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 May' GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Film_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of any cinema that screened more than one film on the date '21 May'.

sql: SELECT T2.Title FROM schedule AS T1 JOIN film AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the title of the film that has the most expensive ticket price in the schedule.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Capacity * SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) AS Total_Daily_Seats FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Cinema_ID, T1.Name, T1.Capacity ORDER BY Total_Daily_Seats DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single cinema with the highest total daily seating capacity, which is calculated by multiplying its capacity by the sum of its daily showtimes.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Directed_by FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'County Wicklow';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the distinct director of films that have been scheduled to show in any cinema located in 'County Wicklow'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING MIN(T3.Rank_in_series) > 25;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of cinemas where every film shown has a series rank greater than 25.

sql: SELECT AVG(Show_times_per_day) FROM schedule WHERE Cinema_ID IN (SELECT Cinema_ID FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year < 2012);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the average number of daily show times for all schedules at cinemas that opened before the year 2012.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Directed_by, T1.Location FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID = T3.Film_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique combinations of film directors and the cinema locations where their films have been screened.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, AVG(T2.Price) AS avg_price FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY avg_price DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single cinema with the highest average schedule price, listing its name and that average price.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 WHERE T1.Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner' EXCEPT SELECT T2.Title FROM schedule AS T1 JOIN film AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the film titles directed by 'Bill Schreiner' that are not scheduled for screening on '21 May'.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by, SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) AS TotalScreenings FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Directed_by;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each film director along with the total number of daily screenings for all films they have directed.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Capacity) AS Total_Capacity FROM cinema GROUP BY Location ORDER BY Total_Capacity DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single location with the highest total cinema capacity, calculated by summing the capacities of all cinemas within that location.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Date) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of a cinema that has showtimes scheduled on more than one distinct day.

sql: SELECT Artist_ID, Artist, Age FROM artist;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the ID, name, and age for every artist in the database.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Age = 35;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the artist name and their famous title for all artists who are 35 years old.

sql: SELECT Song, Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top = 1.0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the song title and artist ID for all songs that spent exactly one week at the top of the charts.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Date_of_ceremony = '18 February 2011';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the music festival and category for all ceremonies held on February 18, 2011.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist ORDER BY Artist ASC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each artist and their corresponding famous title, ordered alphabetically by the artist's name.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist's name and the total number of volumes associated with them.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, SUM(T2.Weeks_on_Top) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artist and the total number of weeks their volumes have spent on top.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING COUNT(T2.Volume_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artists who have released more than one volume.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top > 1.0);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing artists and their famous titles, specifically for those whose releases have spent more than one week at a top chart position.

sql: SELECT T2.Song, T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the song and corresponding artist for all entries that received a 'Nominated' result at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Category = 'Best Song' AND T1.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the artists who have been awarded 'Best Song' at a music festival.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Category FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID WHERE T2.Artist_ID = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the unique music festival categories associated with the artist identified by ID 2.

sql: SELECT T3.Artist, COUNT(T1.ID) FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID GROUP BY T3.Artist HAVING COUNT(T1.ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing artists who have appeared at more than one music festival, along with the total count of their appearances.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > (SELECT AVG(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing artists and their corresponding songs that have remained in the top spot for a longer duration than the average song.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM volume) AND T1.Artist_ID NOT IN (SELECT T3.Artist_ID FROM music_festival AS T2 JOIN volume AS T3 ON T2.Volume = T3.Volume_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Awarded');
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artists who have released a volume but have never received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Music_Festival, T1.Category FROM music_festival AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM volume AS T2 JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Volume_ID = T1.Volume AND T3.Age > 30);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the name and category of music festivals that have featured at least one performing artist over the age of 30.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.Age > 30 UNION SELECT T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the unique names of artists who are either older than 30 or have won an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.Age > 30 INTERSECT SELECT T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Category = 'Best Song' AND T1.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the artists who are both over 30 years old and have been nominated for 'Best Song' at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Nominated' EXCEPT SELECT T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the artist who was nominated for a music festival award but did not win.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Festival_Count FROM artist AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.Artist_ID, COUNT(T1.ID) AS Festival_Count FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID GROUP BY T2.Artist_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows, listing each artist and the total count of music festivals they have participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song, T2.Weeks_on_Top FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = (SELECT MAX(T3.Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume AS T3 WHERE T3.Artist_ID = T1.Artist_ID);
result_summary: Returns 7 rows identifying each artist's song (or songs, in case of a tie) that spent the most weeks on top, listing the artist, song title, and that maximum week count.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Age) FROM artist AS T1 WHERE T1.Artist_ID IN (SELECT T3.Artist_ID FROM music_festival AS T2 JOIN volume AS T3 ON T2.Volume = T3.Volume_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Awarded');
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average age for all artists who have received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Famous_Title, T2.Famous_Title, T1.Age, T2.Age FROM artist AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist = T2.Artist WHERE T1.Artist_ID < T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows comparing different records for the same artist, showing the artist's name alongside a pair of their famous titles and the ages associated with each record.

sql: SELECT T3.Artist, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.Result = 'Awarded' THEN 1 END) AS Awards, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.Result = 'Nominated' THEN 1 END) AS Nominations FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID GROUP BY T3.Artist;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing each artist along with the total number of festival awards won and nominations received.

sql: SELECT Member_ID, Name FROM member WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the Member ID and name for all members from the 'United States'.

sql: SELECT Name, Leader_Name FROM college WHERE College_Location = 'Ottawa';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the college located in 'Ottawa' and the name of its leader.

sql: SELECT Decoration_Theme FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the decoration theme for each round that ranked first.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM member ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing the name and country of each member, ordered alphabetically by the member's name.

sql: SELECT Name, Leader_Name FROM college;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each college by its name and the name of its leader.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each member's name alongside the name of the college they are associated with.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Member_ID) FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of members who have achieved a first-place rank in a round.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(Member_ID) FROM member GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing each country along with the total count of members from that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Decoration_Theme = 'Walk on the Moon';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the member who participated in a round with the 'Walk on the Moon' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, MIN(T2.Rank_in_Round) FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing each member's name alongside their best rank ever achieved in a single round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 WHERE T1.College_ID IN (SELECT T2.College_ID FROM member AS T2 WHERE T2.Country = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of colleges that have members from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T3.Rank_in_Round FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID JOIN round AS T3 ON T1.Member_ID = T3.Member_ID WHERE T3.Decoration_Theme = 'Walk on the Moon';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the member, their college, and their rank in the round specifically for the event with the 'Walk on the Moon' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Member_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of colleges that have more than one member.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round) AND T1.Member_ID NOT IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 1);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of members who have participated in at least one round but have never achieved a first-place rank.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM college AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Member_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the college that has the most members.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Country = 'United States' INTERSECT SELECT T2.Name FROM member AS T2 WHERE T2.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round);

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of captains who command ships registered under the Panamanian flag.

sql: SELECT Rank, COUNT(*) FROM captain GROUP BY Rank;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each captain rank along with the total number of captains holding that rank.

sql: SELECT Rank, COUNT(*) FROM captain GROUP BY Rank HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the military ranks that are held by more than one captain, along with the total number of captains holding each of those ranks.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Type;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each ship type along with the total number of captains assigned to ships of that type.

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM Ship WHERE Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name and type for each ship that currently has a captain assigned.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T2.Built_Year FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID ORDER BY T1.age DESC;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each captain's name, the name of their assigned ship, and the ship's built year, ordered from the oldest captain to the youngest.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and age of captains who are older than the average age of all captains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM Ship AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM captain AS T2 WHERE T2.Ship_ID = T1.Ship_ID AND T2.age > 50);
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and type for each ship that is commanded by at least one captain over the age of 50.

sql: SELECT T2.Flag, COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Flag HAVING COUNT(T1.Captain_ID) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each ship's flag that is associated with more than two captains, along with the total count of captains for that flag.

sql: SELECT T3.Flag, AVG(T3.captain_age) FROM (SELECT T1.age AS captain_age, T2.Flag FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.Flag;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing each ship's flag along with the average age of captains for ships registered under that flag.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Ship_ID NOT IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship WHERE Built_Year = 1997.0);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the names of captains who do not command a ship built in the year 1997.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain UNION SELECT Name FROM Ship;
result_summary: Returns 16 rows providing a single, unique list containing the names of all captains and ships.

sql: SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain WHERE age > 40 INTERSECT SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship WHERE Type = 'Panamax';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the Ship IDs for 'Panamax' type vessels that are also commanded by a captain over the age of 40.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain EXCEPT SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and age of all captains, excluding those who command a 'Third-rate ship of the line'.

sql: SELECT T2.Class, AVG(T1.age) FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama' GROUP BY T2.Class;
result_summary: Returns 1 row calculating the average age of captains for each class of ship that flies the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM captain);
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name and age of the oldest captain.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Rank FROM captain AS T1 WHERE T1.Ship_ID IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM Ship WHERE Built_Year = (SELECT MIN(Built_Year) FROM Ship));
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the name and rank of captains who command the oldest ship or ships, as determined by the earliest build year.

sql: SELECT T2.Type FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID GROUP BY T2.Type HAVING AVG(T1.age) > 44;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the ship type(s) for which the average age of the captains is greater than 44.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN Ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line' AND T2.Type = 'Panamax';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of captains with the class 'Third-rate ship of the line' who command 'Panamax' type ships.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Location FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the name and location for each festival held in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of artworks that are classified as 'Program Music/Variety Show'.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail ORDER BY Num_of_Audience DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing festival names and their corresponding audience size, ordered from the largest audience to the smallest.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM artwork;
result_summary: Returns 1 row providing a single value for the total number of artworks in the database.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Result FROM nomination;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the distinct possible outcomes for a nomination, such as 'Nominated' or 'Won'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of all artworks that have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each festival's name along with the total number of artworks nominated for that festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(*) FROM artwork AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Type;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing each artwork type along with the total count of artworks for that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Nominated' AND T1.Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of festivals located in the United States that have received a nomination.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks that were nominated for any festival held in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name HAVING SUM(T1.Num_of_Audience) > 150;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of festivals that had a total audience count of over 150 people.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Year = 2006 UNION SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique names of festivals that either occurred in the year 2006 or were located in the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the name of the festival that has received the most artwork nominations.

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.Result) = 2;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of festivals that have had nominations with exactly two distinct outcomes.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the artwork that won an award at the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT Chair_Name, Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Num_of_Audience > (SELECT AVG(Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the chair and festival names for festivals that had an audience count greater than the average across all festivals.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID NOT IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all artworks that have not won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won' GROUP BY T1.Type ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single artwork type that has won the most nominations.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM artwork AS T2 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T1.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID AND T3.Location = 'United States');
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the names of all artworks that received a nomination at a festival located in the United States.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_ID IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination) AND Festival_ID NOT IN (SELECT Festival_ID FROM nomination WHERE Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the festival that has received nominations but has never won an award.

sql: SELECT T1.Year, T1.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE T1.Num_of_Audience = (SELECT MAX(T2.Num_of_Audience) FROM festival_detail AS T2 WHERE T2.Year = T1.Year);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows, listing the year and the name of the festival that had the largest audience for each respective year.

sql: SELECT T3.Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID JOIN artwork AS T3 ON T2.Artwork_ID = T3.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Chair_Name = 'Raymond Floyd' AND T1.Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of artworks nominated for festivals that were located in the United States and chaired by 'Raymond Floyd'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T1.Type = T2.Type AND T1.Artwork_ID < T2.Artwork_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows, listing all unique pairs of artworks that share the same `Type`.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN artwork AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Won' AND T1.Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007 ORDER BY Num_of_Audience DESC LIMIT 1);

sql: SELECT T1.Festival_Name, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Result = 'Won' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Wins, SUM(CASE WHEN T2.Result = 'Nominated' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Nominations FROM festival_detail AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T1.Festival_Name HAVING COUNT(T2.Artwork_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing festivals that have received more than one nomination, showing a total count of their wins and nominations.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 34 rows listing the first and last name for every student in the database.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE StuID = 1001;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing all available profile details for the student with an ID of 1001.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Has_Allergy WHERE StuID = 1002;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the specific allergies for the student with an ID of 1002.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing the names of all allergies that are classified as a 'food' allergy type.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the total count of all students in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 58 rows listing the first and last name of each student alongside each of their specific allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, COUNT(T2.Allergy) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID;
result_summary: Returns 29 rows, listing the first and last name of each student along with a count of their total recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.AllergyType, COUNT(T2.Allergy) FROM Allergy_Type AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy GROUP BY T1.AllergyType;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each allergy type and the total number of recorded allergies corresponding to that type.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T1.city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of students who have a recorded allergy and are from the city with code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT avg(Age) FROM Student;
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the average age of all students.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > 18;
result_summary: Returns 19 rows listing the first name, last name, and age for all students who are older than 18.

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(*) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each major and the total number of students enrolled in it.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy, T3.AllergyType FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy;
result_summary: Returns 58 rows listing the first and last name of each student, their specific allergy, and the corresponding general type of that allergy.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT T1.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy WHERE T2.AllergyType = 'food');
result_summary: Returns 14 rows containing the first and last names of all students who have a food allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.Allergy) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing the first and last names of students who have more than one recorded allergy.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy);
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the first and last names of all students who do not have any recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT StuID FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'HKG' INTERSECT SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy WHERE Allergy = 'Shellfish';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the ID of the student who is from Hong Kong ('HKG') and also has a shellfish allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID EXCEPT SELECT StuID, Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the student ID, first name, and last name for all non-female students who are registered as having an allergy.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Allergy, (SELECT AVG(T3.Age) FROM Student AS T3 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T4 ON T3.StuID = T4.StuID WHERE T4.Allergy = T1.Allergy) FROM Has_Allergy AS T1;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each distinct allergy along with the average age of the students who have that allergy.

sql: SELECT T3.AllergyType FROM (SELECT T1.StuID, T2.AllergyType FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.AllergyType ORDER BY COUNT(T3.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single most common allergy type among all students, determined by finding which allergy is associated with the highest number of students.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Returns 15 rows listing the first name, last name, and age of students who are older than the average age of all students.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL' UNION SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Shellfish';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all students who either live in Baltimore (city code 'BAL') or have a shellfish allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food' GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING count(*) >= 1;
result_summary: Returns 14 rows listing the first and last names of students who have at least one food-related allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID AND T2.Allergy = 'Cat');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the first and last names of all students who have a recorded allergy to cats.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, AVG(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY StuID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows detailing the average age of students with multiple allergies, broken down by gender.

sql: SELECT customer_id, first_name, last_name FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good';
result_summary: Returns 11 rows containing the ID, first name, and last name of all customers who are classified as 'good'.

sql: SELECT product_name, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product name and daily hire cost for all products categorized as cutlery.

sql: SELECT payment_id, booking_id, amount_paid FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the payment ID, booking ID, and amount paid for all transactions that were settled with cash.

sql: SELECT booking_id, customer_id, booking_start_date, amount_payable FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing all confirmed bookings, showing the booking ID, customer ID, start date, and the amount payable for each.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_description, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire ORDER BY daily_hire_cost DESC;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the name, description, and daily hire cost for all available products, ordered from the most to the least expensive.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.booking_status_code, T2.booking_start_date FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T1.customer_id = 7;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows detailing all bookings for a specific customer (ID 7), including the customer's name, booking status code, and the start date of each booking.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, SUM(T2.amount_paid) AS total_amount_paid FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id GROUP BY T1.booking_id;
result_summary: Returns 10 rows listing each unique booking ID along with the total amount paid for that booking, calculated by summing all associated payments.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS number_of_bookings FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing each product's name along with the total number of times it has been booked.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS total_bookings FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id;
result_summary: Returns 7 rows listing each customer's first and last name, along with the total number of bookings they have made.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Discount_Coupons AS T2 ON T1.coupon_id = T2.coupon_id WHERE T2.coupon_amount > (SELECT AVG(coupon_amount) FROM Discount_Coupons);
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the first and last names of customers whose discount coupon value is greater than the average coupon amount.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T2.status_date, T2.available_yn FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN View_Product_Availability AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T1.product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the product name, last status update date, and current availability for all products classified as 'Cutlery'.

sql: SELECT T1.booking_id, T1.booking_status_code, T2.payment_type_code FROM Bookings AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.booking_id = T2.booking_id WHERE T2.amount_paid_in_full_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 9 rows for bookings that have been paid in full, listing each booking's ID, its current status, and the payment method used.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.returned_damaged_yn = '1';
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the unique first and last names of all customers who have returned a damaged item.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.amount_paid) AS total_paid FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Payments AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each customer's first and last name, along with the total amount they have paid across all of their bookings.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'Book collection C';
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the unique first and last names of all customers who have booked the product named 'Book collection C'.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Products_Booked AS T2 WHERE T2.returned_late_yn = '1');
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the product name and daily hire cost for all products that have been returned late at least once.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, SUM(T2.booked_count) AS total_items_booked FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_name HAVING COUNT(T2.booking_id) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing products that have been booked more than once, showing the total quantity of items booked for each of those products.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products_Booked AS T2 JOIN Bookings AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T2.product_id = T1.product_id AND T3.booking_status_code = 'Provisional');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of products for hire that are included in bookings with a 'Provisional' status.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Payments AS T3 ON T2.booking_id = T3.booking_id WHERE T2.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' AND T3.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card';
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the first and last name of the distinct customer who has a confirmed booking that was paid for with a credit card.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.returned_damaged_yn = '1' UNION SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Cash';
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing the first and last names of all customers who have either returned a damaged item or have paid with cash.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Bookings AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' INTERSECT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Payments AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.payment_type_code = 'Check';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have both a confirmed booking and have made a payment by check.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Bookings) EXCEPT SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 WHERE T1.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Payments);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of any customer who has made a booking but has not yet made a corresponding payment.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, AVG(T1.daily_hire_cost) AS avg_cost, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS booking_count FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_type_code HAVING AVG(T1.daily_hire_cost) > 20;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the product type, average daily hire cost, and total booking count for product categories where the average hire cost is greater than $20.

sql: SELECT T1.product_name, T1.daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN (SELECT product_id, COUNT(*) AS booking_count FROM Products_Booked GROUP BY product_id) AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id ORDER BY T2.booking_count DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the product name and daily hire cost for the single most frequently booked product.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, COUNT(T2.TrackId) AS NumberOfTracks FROM Album AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.AlbumId = T2.AlbumId GROUP BY T1.Title;
result_summary: Returns 347 rows, listing each album title along with a count of the total number of tracks it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId;
result_summary: Returns 59 rows, listing each customer's first and last name along with the total amount they have spent across all their invoices.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Genre AS T1 JOIN Track AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId GROUP BY T1.Name ORDER BY TrackCount DESC;
result_summary: Returns 25 rows listing each music genre and its total number of associated tracks, ordered from the genre with the most tracks to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName AS EmployeeFirstName, T1.LastName AS EmployeeLastName, T2.FirstName AS ManagerFirstName, T2.LastName AS ManagerLastName FROM Employee AS T1 LEFT JOIN Employee AS T2 ON T1.ReportsTo = T2.EmployeeId;
result_summary: Returns 8 rows listing each employee's first and last name alongside the first and last name of their direct manager, outlining the company's reporting hierarchy.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS TrackName, T3.Title AS AlbumTitle FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Album AS T3 ON T1.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId JOIN Artist AS T2 ON T3.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId WHERE T2.Name = 'AC/DC';
result_summary: Returns 18 rows listing all tracks by the artist 'AC/DC', showing each track's name and its corresponding album title.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T2.Quantity) AS TotalTracksSold FROM MediaType AS T1 JOIN Track AS T3 ON T1.MediaTypeId = T3.MediaTypeId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T2 ON T3.TrackId = T2.TrackId GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the total quantity of tracks sold for each distinct media type.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS PlaylistName, COUNT(T2.TrackId) AS NumberOfTracks FROM Playlist AS T1 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T2 ON T1.PlaylistId = T2.PlaylistId WHERE T1.Name = 'Music' GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name of the 'Music' playlist and the total count of tracks it contains.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, SUM(T2.Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Customer AS T1 JOIN Invoice AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId = T2.CustomerId GROUP BY T1.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows showing the first name, last name, and total spending for the top 5 customers, ordered from highest to lowest spending.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId WHERE T2.Name = 'Rock' INTERSECT SELECT T3.Name FROM Track AS T3 JOIN PlaylistTrack AS T4 ON T3.TrackId = T4.TrackId JOIN Playlist AS T5 ON T4.PlaylistId = T5.PlaylistId WHERE T5.Name = 'Music';
result_summary: Returns 1213 rows, listing the names of all 'Rock' genre tracks that are also found on the 'Music' playlist.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 WHERE T1.ArtistId IN (SELECT T3.ArtistId FROM Track AS T2 JOIN Album AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId WHERE T2.GenreId = 1) AND T1.ArtistId IN (SELECT T3.ArtistId FROM Track AS T2 JOIN Album AS T3 ON T2.AlbumId = T3.AlbumId WHERE T2.GenreId = 2);
error_message: Discarded: Executed successfully but returned 0 rows.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Track AS T1 WHERE T1.TrackId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT T2.TrackId FROM InvoiceLine AS T2);
result_summary: Returns 1519 rows listing the names of all music tracks that have never been purchased or appeared on an invoice.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName, T1.Email FROM Customer AS T1 WHERE T1.SupportRepId = (SELECT EmployeeId FROM Employee WHERE FirstName = 'Jane' AND LastName = 'Peacock');
result_summary: Returns 21 rows listing the first name, last name, and email for all customers assigned to the support representative Jane Peacock.

sql: SELECT T1.BillingCountry, COUNT(T1.InvoiceId) AS NumberOfInvoices, AVG(T1.Total) AS AverageInvoiceAmount FROM Invoice AS T1 GROUP BY T1.BillingCountry HAVING COUNT(T1.InvoiceId) > 2;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows listing each billing country that has more than two invoices, along with the total invoice count and the average invoice amount for that country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T2 ON T1.ArtistId = T2.ArtistId GROUP BY T1.ArtistId HAVING COUNT(T2.AlbumId) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 56 rows listing the names of artists who have released more than one album.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Customer AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Invoice AS T2 WHERE T2.CustomerId = T1.CustomerId AND T2.Total > 5);
result_summary: Returns 59 rows listing the first and last names of customers who have at least one invoice with a total greater than 5.

sql: SELECT T4.FirstName, T4.LastName, COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS RockTracksPurchased FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Invoice AS T5 ON T3.InvoiceId = T5.InvoiceId JOIN Customer AS T4 ON T5.CustomerId = T4.CustomerId WHERE T2.Name = 'Rock' GROUP BY T4.CustomerId ORDER BY RockTracksPurchased DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single customer who has purchased the most 'Rock' genre tracks, displaying their first name, last name, and the total count of those purchases.

sql: SELECT T3.Title AS AlbumTitle FROM Artist AS T1 JOIN Album AS T3 ON T1.ArtistId = T3.ArtistId JOIN Track AS T2 ON T3.AlbumId = T2.AlbumId JOIN MediaType AS T4 ON T2.MediaTypeId = T4.MediaTypeId WHERE T1.Name = 'Accept' AND T4.Name = 'Protected AAC audio file';
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the album titles by the artist 'Accept' that contain tracks in the 'Protected AAC audio file' format.

sql: SELECT T1.FirstName, T1.LastName FROM Employee AS T1 WHERE T1.EmployeeId IN (SELECT T2.SupportRepId FROM Customer AS T2 GROUP BY T2.SupportRepId HAVING COUNT(T2.CustomerId) > 1);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the first and last names of employees who act as support representatives for more than one customer.

sql: SELECT Country, GenreName, TracksSold FROM (SELECT T5.BillingCountry AS Country, T2.Name AS GenreName, COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS TracksSold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T5.BillingCountry ORDER BY COUNT(T1.TrackId) DESC) as rn FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Invoice AS T5 ON T3.InvoiceId = T5.InvoiceId GROUP BY T5.BillingCountry, T2.Name) AS T6 WHERE T6.rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows identifying the most popular music genre within each country, based on the total number of tracks sold.

sql: SELECT Name, Sex FROM people;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the name and sex for all people in the dataset.

sql: SELECT * FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing all polling details for candidates who were included in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people WHERE Height = 188.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row listing the name and height of any person who is exactly 188.0 units tall.

sql: SELECT Candidate_ID, Support_rate FROM candidate WHERE Support_rate = 0.25;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the candidate who has a support rate of exactly 25%, listing their ID and that specific rate.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Poll_Source FROM candidate;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each unique poll source found in the candidate data.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Poll_Source FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing the name of each candidate along with their corresponding poll source.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, COUNT(Candidate_ID) FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows, listing each poll source along with the total number of candidates associated with that source.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each poll source along with the average candidate support rate calculated from that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Date = 'Feb 12–15, 2007';
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of candidates associated with the date 'Feb 12–15, 2007'.

sql: SELECT T1.Sex, COUNT(T2.Candidate_ID) FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Sex;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows showing the total count of political candidates for each sex.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, MAX(Oppose_rate) FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing each polling source along with the maximum opposition rate recorded for that source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 LEFT JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Candidate_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Returns 3 rows listing the names of people who are not registered as candidates.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate = (SELECT MAX(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the candidate who has the highest support rate.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source HAVING COUNT(Candidate_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the poll sources that have more than one candidate associated with them.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Support_rate > (SELECT AVG(T3.Support_rate) FROM candidate AS T3 WHERE T3.Poll_Source = T2.Poll_Source);
result_summary: Returns 2 rows listing the names of candidates whose support rate is greater than the average support rate for their specific poll source.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Height > (SELECT AVG(Height) FROM people);
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the name and height for candidates whose height is greater than the overall average height.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' AND T2.Support_rate < (SELECT AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'FOX News/Opinion Dynamics Poll');
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a candidate from the 'WNBC/Marist Poll' whose support rate is below the average support rate of candidates from the 'FOX News/Opinion Dynamics Poll'.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source ORDER BY AVG(Unsure_rate) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single poll source that has the highest average unsure rate among its respondents.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM candidate) UNION SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 190;
result_summary: Returns 9 rows listing the unique names of people who are either registered as a candidate or have a height greater than 190.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll' EXCEPT SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of a male candidate who was included in the 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source HAVING AVG(Support_rate) > (SELECT AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the poll source where the average candidate support rate is greater than the overall average support rate across all polls.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight > (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M');
result_summary: Returns 4 rows listing the names of candidates whose weight is greater than the average weight of all males.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Returns 5 rows listing the names of all individuals who are both male and registered as a candidate.

sql: SELECT T1.Poll_Source, T3.Name FROM candidate AS T1 JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.People_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T1.Support_rate = (SELECT MAX(T2.Support_rate) FROM candidate AS T2 WHERE T2.Poll_Source = T1.Poll_Source);
result_summary: Returns 3 rows identifying the candidate with the highest support rate for each unique poll source, listing the poll source and that candidate's name.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Sex, COUNT(T2.Candidate_ID) AS Poll_Count FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.People_ID, T1.Name, T1.Sex HAVING COUNT(T2.Candidate_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Returns 6 rows listing each person who is a candidate, their sex, and the total number of polls they have participated in.
